questions.prompt: Multiple Choice: Answer all three parts (a), (b), (c) about algorithm complexity.
questions.modelAnswer: Multiple Choice Answers:n(a) A, D, En(b) Only An(c) A, D
questions.gradingCriteria: Award full credit for exact matches. Partial credit 15 pts each: part (a), part (b), part (c).

questions.prompt: Algorithm Design: Describe an O(n) divide-and-conquer algorithm for max sub-array product.
questions.modelAnswer: Divide array into two halves, recursively find max sub-array product, max/min prefix/suffix products, and total product for each half. Combine results: maxProd = max(LmaxProd, RmaxProd, LmaxSuff*RmaxPre, LminSuff*RminPre). Running time: O(n), recurrence T(n) = 2T(n/2) + O(1).
questions.gradingCriteria: Key elements: (1) divide-and-conquer structure 10pts, (2) correct combining step 15pts, (3) correct O(n) runtime analysis 10pts.

questions.prompt: Proof by Induction: Prove that g(n) = O(log log n) using strong induction.
questions.modelAnswer: Define g(n) recursively, base cases n <= 4. Inductive Hypothesis: g(n) <= log log n + 1. Use strong induction, apply hypothesis to g(floor(sqrt(n))), conclude g(n) = O(log log n).
questions.gradingCriteria: Key elements: (1) correct base cases 5pts, (2) correct inductive hypothesis 5pts, (3) valid inductive step with sqrt argument 10pts.
